title: bixa arborea
intro: Bixa arborea, also known as the Brazil nut tree or achiote, is a small evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its seeds, which contain a bright orange-red pigment called bixin, a carotenoid with potential medicinal properties. The seeds of Bixa arborea have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from fever, wounds, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Bixa arborea, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
